customer_anxiety_profile: Nora is a compliant but not committed booker. Her trust is social-proof-dependent — she references her son's Duke Power experience and her neighbor's panels as validators. She is older, splits time between her home and her son's family, and is not highly tech-literate. She agreed to the appointment without strong conviction; her warmest moment was the ground-mount question at the end, suggesting genuine curiosity about her property's options.
unresolved_concerns: Tree shading on the west side of the property — Nora flagged tall trees with seasonal leaf cover as a potential disqualifier. Neighbor confirmed this concern.
Social-proof gap — Nora wanted to talk to her neighbor before committing. She booked without doing so. The closer should expect she may have spoken to the neighbor overnight.
Ground-mount feasibility — Nora asked about field-mounted panels, suggesting the roof may not be her preferred installation site.
sensitive_topics_raised: Age and semi-dependent living situation — Nora stays with her son's family frequently and is not always at the home.
Utility bill variability — Nora's bill ranges from $150–$250 normally but spiked to $350 in March due to an unattended thermostat. She is aware of the spike and slightly embarrassed by it.
exact_promises_rep_made: Phone call appointment Tuesday at 12:00 PM.
Ion Solar has been in business since 2013 and 'has never been in debt once.'
The specialist will be able to discuss tree-trimming options.
The specialist will be able to answer the ground-mount / field installation question.
closer_should_know: Nora's booking is passive — she said 'sure' and 'if you wanna give me a quote' rather than expressing genuine enthusiasm. Her trust anchor is her neighbor (who already has solar panels). Open with the neighbor reference and use it to validate rather than compete: 'your neighbor can speak to the experience; I'm here to show you what the numbers look like for your specific property.' The tree-shading concern and ground-mount question both suggest the roof may be a problem — come prepared to discuss alternative configurations. Do not rely on the $310 bill figure being emotionally live for Nora; Bucky captured it but never anchored it, so the closer should re-establish bill pain early.
